codeReview.summary: Feature intent is implemented: the commit adds direct VAL_EXEC_009 scheduler regression lock-in tests for non-running completion/failure rejection and create()/activeScheduler registration semantics, and updates runtime-orchestration-worker guidance to distinguish canonical class filters from supplemental assertion-filter commands.

sharedStateObservations.observation: runtime-orchestration-worker procedure text and observed handoff behavior are still misaligned for `followedProcedure`. The skill says `followedProcedure=true` requires evidence for every required Branch A validator command, but this handoff marks `followedProcedure=true` while only one canonical suite plus assertion filters were run.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/runtime-orchestration-worker/SKILL.md:39 and :48 require all Branch A validators for `followedProcedure=true`; handoff 2026-04-21T00-27-54-579Z...31012dbe...json:16 (VAL_EXEC_009), :31 (BurnBarMissionControlServiceTests), :66-67 (`followedProcedure": true`, `deviations": []`)

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission guidance still describes `.factory/services.yaml` as the single source of truth, while assertion-filter usage is now allowed via skill guidance but not represented in services command definitions, which can still create operator ambiguity.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/albertonunez/.factory/missions/d1d1287f-f2a9-4a34-8ba9-fce37df13493/AGENTS.md:29; .factory/services.yaml has no `assertion-filter`/`VAL_EXEC` entries; .factory/skills/runtime-orchestration-worker/SKILL.md:62-69 introduces supplemental assertion-filter usage.

summary: Reviewed commit 9215a514* for misc-m11 and found the implementation complete for the requested regression lock-ins and guidance update, with no blocking code issues. Recorded shared-state alignment gaps around validator-procedure wording versus observed handoff behavior and services-source-of-truth guidance.
